name: Search Commands
guide_hints:
# Level 14: Search Commands

While `auto` is great for single-step deductions, sometimes a proof requires chaining multiple theorems together. This is where `search`, `backward_search`, and `advanced_search` come in.
These commands perform a Graph Search through the space of possible logical deductions, trying to find a path from your proven premises to your goal.

**Disclaimer:** It is highly recommended NOT to use these commands for learning purposes, as they defeat the point of practicing logical deduction. Furthermore, these search algorithms are currently quite basic and can be slow for complex proofs.
There is a massive amount of scope for improvement in these algorithms! If you are interested in Graph Theory or AI, you can contribute by submitting Pull Requests to the open-source repository.

### Instructions:
Create prop variables `p`, `q`, `r`.
Create the Syllogism goal: `cf goal ((p ⇒ q) ∧ (q ⇒ r)) ⇒ (p ⇒ r)`.

Start a mission: `mission goal`.
Type `left <premise>` and `right <premise>` to extract `p ⇒ q` and `q ⇒ r`.
Then type `search goal`. The prover will explore the graph and find the proof chaining them together!
